title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bondel and Detective Inspector Bartholomew, currently disguised as a butler, discuss the necessity of discretion to avoid alerting potential thieves to their presence. George Lomax informs Bondel that Mrs. McCarta is unable to attend the gala because her child has mumps. George introduces the Countess Razky, a wealthy Hungarian leader of the Young Hungarian Party dedicated to public service and reducing infant mortality, as a special guest. Bondel meets Hel Eberhard, a nervous German inventor, before Bill Everly arrives, appearing exhausted from his duties entertaining the Countess. Jimmy Sycamore reveals that the Countess intends to have Bill take her to an interesting old location following tea, leading Bondel to express concern regarding Bill's ability to manage her.

After dinner, the ladies gather in the drawing room of the Two-Footed Dragon Church. Because the room lacks central heating, the Countess, Lady Cutt, and Bondel struggle with the cold. During their conversation, the women discuss the mumps, and Bondel provides a vivid, exaggerated account of the misery in post-war Budapest. The Countess uses these stories to argue that Hungary suffers from a lack of organization rather than a lack of money. Lady Cutt reflects on her husband Sir Oswald’s success, attributing it to his meticulous and methodical nature. The gathering concludes with Lady Cutt asking Bondel about the head gardener, McDonald, noting with envy that McDonald follows Bondel's orders.
